revision.comment: Bot: mark 2 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24155476638|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:長持形石棺]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Reproduction of a long chest-shaped Sarcophagus excavated from {{ill|Ofujiyama Kofun|ja|お富士山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofujiyama Kofun|qq=}}
({{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|en|National Museum of Japanese History|qid=Q3330690|lt=National Museum of Japanese History|1=National Museum of Japanese History}})]] '''Long chest-shaped Sarcophagus''' (長持形石棺, Nagamochigata Sekkan) is a type of composite {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} that appeared during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. == Overview == This type of box-type Sarcophagus is composed of six stone slabs: a base stone, two long side stones, two short side stones, and a lid stone. Its shape resembles a {{ill|Nagamochi|lt=Nagamochi|lt=Long chest|ja|長持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long chest|qq=}}, which gives it its name. The short side stones and the upper part of the lid stone are rounded in a semicircular shape. Additionally, the lid stone, long side stones, and base stone have {{ill|rope attachment projections|ja|縄掛突起|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rope attachment projections|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135551150}}. These coffins are commonly found in large-scale kofun in the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|qid=Q906708|lt=Kinai region|1=Kinai}}, and in other areas, they are used for the tombs of local rulers. The Kinai examples are made from {{ill|rhyolitic welded tuff|ja|流紋岩質溶結凝灰岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rhyolitic welded tuff|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135551151}}, also known as {{ill|Tatsuyama stone|ja|竜山石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuyama stone|qq=}}, sourced from the {{ill|Tatsuyama quarry|ja|竜山石採石遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuyama quarry|qq=}} in {{ill|Takasago, Hyōgo|en|Takasago, Hyōgo|qid=Q869037|lt=Takasago, Hyōgo|1=Takasago, Hyōgo}}. == Kofun with Long Chest-Shaped Stone Coffins == *Bold names indicate use of Tatsuyama stone. === Kanto Region === * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|en|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qid=Q11444167|lt=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun}} – {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|qid=Q386179|lt=Ōta, Gunma|1=Ōta, Gunma}}, Gunma Prefecture, 210 meters * {{ill|Ofujiyama Kofun|ja|お富士山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofujiyama Kofun|qq=}} – {{ill|Isesaki|en|Isesaki|qid=Q328596|lt=Isesaki, Gunma|1=Isesaki}}, Gunma Prefecture, 125 meters * Other examples include composite Sarcophaguss similar to long chest-shaped ones from {{ill|Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Sannobunme Ōtsukayama Kofun|lt=Sannobunme Ōtsukayama Kofun|lt=San-no-fun-me Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|三ノ分目大塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San-no-fun-me Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}}. === Kinai Region === * '''{{ill|Muro Miyayama Kofun|en|Muro Miyayama Kofun|qid=Q11452994|lt=Muro Miyayama Kofun|1=Muro Miyayama Kofun}}''' – {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|qid=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara|1=Gose, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, 238 meters, lattice pattern engraving on lid * '''{{ill|Yashikiyama Kofun|en|Yashikiyama Kofun|qid=Q105092557|lt=Yashikiyama Kofun|1=Yashikiyama Kofun}}''' – {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|en|Katsuragi, Nara|qid=Q659672|lt=Katsuragi, Nara|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, 145 meters * '''{{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|lt=Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|lt=Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|qid=Q696643|lt=Kashiba, Nara|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, 140 meters * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|qid=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun|1=Kushiyama Kofun}} – {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara|1=Tenri, Nara}}, square-mound tomb, 152 meters * '''{{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|lt=Maetsuka Kofun|lt=Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki)|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki)|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|qid=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki, Osaka|1=Takatsuki}}, 95 meters * '''{{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|qid=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}, 225 meters * '''{{ill|Shionjiyama Kofun|en|Shionjiyama Kofun|qid=Q11490575|lt=Shionjiyama Kofun|1=Shionjiyama Kofun}}''' – {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|qid=Q490872|lt=Yao, Osaka|1=Yao, Osaka}}, 160 meters * {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} – {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujidera, Osaka|1=Fujiidera}}, 208 meters, lattice pattern engraving on lid * {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} – {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|qid=Q193428|lt=Sakai, Osaka|1=Sakai}}, 486 meters * {{ill|Chin'ooka Kofun|ja|乳岡古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichio Kofun|qq=}} – Sakai, Osaka, 150 meters * {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|qid=Q11629965|lt=Sairyō Kofun|1=Sairyō Kofun}} - Osaka Prefecture, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|qid=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District|1=Sennan District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|qid=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town|1=Misaki, Osaka}}, 210 meters * '''{{ill|Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}''' - {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|qid=Q817276|lt=Joyo City|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, 156 meters * {{ill|Ubusunayama Kofun|en|Ubusunayama Kofun|qid=Q103960471|lt=Ubusunayama Kofun|1=Ubusunayama Kofun}} - Kyoto Prefecture, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|qid=Q513897|lt=Kyotango City|1=Kyōtango}}, round tumulus, diameter 50 meters * {{ill|Riko Kofun|ja|離胡古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Riko Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kyotango City, square tumulus, side length 30 meters * Baba-no-Uchi Kofun - Kyoto Prefecture, Kyotango City, tumulus shape and size unknown * {{ill|Ho-oji Kofun|ja|法王寺古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ho-oji Kofun|qq=}} - Kyoto Prefecture, {{ill|Yosa District, Kyoto|en|Yosa District, Kyoto|qid=Q1078639|lt=Yosa District|1=Yosa District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|en|Yosano, Kyoto|qid=Q1203259|lt=Yosano Town|1=Yosano, Kyoto}}, 74 meters * {{ill|Kumbe Kurazuka Kofun|lt=Kumbe Kurazuka Kofun|lt=Kumo-be Kurazuka Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumo-be Kurazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kumo-be {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) - {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama City]], 140 meters * {{ill|Tamaoka Kofun Cluster|lt=Tamaoka Kofun|ja|玉丘古墳群#玉丘古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaoka Kofun Cluster|1=Tamaoka Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572357}} - Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|qid=Q851155|lt=Kasai City|1=Kasai, Hyōgo}}, 105 meters, features lattice-like carvings on the lid * {{ill|Danjōzan Kofun|en|Danjōzan Kofun|qid=Q22119379|lt=Danjōzan Kofun|1=Danjōzan Kofun}} - Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji City|1=Himeji}}, 140 meters * {{ill|Danjōzan Kofun|lt=Yamano Koshi Kofun|ja|山之越古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjōzan Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 壇場山古墳}} - Hyōgo Prefecture, Himeji City, square tumulus, side length 56 meters * In addition to the above, there are approximately 10 tumuli whose locations are unknown. Additionally, {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hōraiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|qid=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun|1=Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun}} are known from historical traditions of their existence. === Chugoku Region === * '''{{ill|Mori-yama Kofun|lt=Mori-yama Kofun|lt=Shusenda Kofun|ja|朱千駄古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusenda Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 両宮山古墳群}}''' – {{ill|Akaiwa|en|Akaiwa|qid=Q415372|lt=Akaiwa, Okayama|1=Akaiwa}}, 80 meters * {{ill|Shōsenzuka Kofun|ja|正仙塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsenzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Tsuyama|en|Tsuyama|qid=Q841125|lt=Tsuyama, Okayama|1=Tsuyama}}, 56 meters === Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Tsukioka Kofun|ja|月岡古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Kofun|qq=}} – {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|en|Ukiha, Fukuoka|qid=Q850373|lt=Ukiha, Fukuoka|1=Ukiha, Fukuoka}}, 95 meters * {{ill|Taniguchi Kofun|ja|谷口古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|qid=Q738275|lt=Karatsu, Saga|1=Karatsu, Saga}}, square-mound tomb, 77 meters == Facilities Displaying Long Chest-Shaped Stone Coffins == * {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|en|National Museum of Japanese History|qid=Q3330690|lt=National Museum of Japanese History|1=National Museum of Japanese History}} – Reproduction from Ofujiyama Kofun * {{ill|Kashiba City Futakami Cultural Center|ja|香芝市ふたかみ文化センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiba City Futakami Cultural Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Coffin lid from Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun == Gallery == File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|Sarcophagus from {{ill|Ofujiyama Kofun|ja|お富士山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofujiyama Kofun|qq=}} File:Hououji Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus lid from {{ill|Ho-oji Kofun|ja|法王寺古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ho-oji Kofun|qq=}} == References == * Tadahiko Makabe, "Thinking About the Kofun Period Through Stone Coffins," Dōbōsha Publishing, January 1994 * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Gōda, "Basic Terms for Archaeological Exploration," Yamakawa Publishing, July 2000 == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} * {{ill|Sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|en|Matsuokayama Kofun|qid=Q11530088|lt=Matsuokayama Kofun|1=Matsuokayama Kofun}} * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun Period|1=Kofun period}} * {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|qid=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} == External Links == * [http://kenhaku.pref.okayama.jp/cgi/lgn/MetDefault.exe?IS_STYLE=default&DEF_XSL=default&SPARA=false&CHG_MAIN=kouko Digital Museum of Okayama Prefecture] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持形石棺|version=91926759|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:長持形石棺]] {{wikidata link|Q17221527}} [[Category:古墳]] [[Category:日本の考古資料]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:遺構]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
